acceptanceCriteria: A new module at apps/web/src/lib/prepare-for-synthesis.ts exports SynthesisChunk interface and prepareForSynthesis function with the specified signature
Calling prepareForSynthesis(text, maxLength) normalizes the input via normalizeText first, then splits with chunkText using the given maxLength
Each returned SynthesisChunk includes wordCount computed via the existing wordCount helper on that chunk's text
Empty string or whitespace-only input returns an empty array []
A maxLength <= 0 throws (delegating to chunkText's existing guard)
Every chunk's text.length is <= maxLength (inherited from chunkText's guarantee)
The chunks' text values, when rejoined in order, reproduce the normalized input's content exactly
The implementation imports and calls chunkText, normalizeText, and wordCount — no duplicated logic
A vitest test suite covers: empty/whitespace input, single chunk under limit, multi-chunk splitting, correct per-chunk wordCount, maxLength invariant per chunk, and non-positive maxLength throwing
npm run build:web and npm run test both pass with zero failures
outOfScope: Wiring prepareForSynthesis into the dispatch/HTTP path or any provider integration
Modifying chunkText, normalizeText, or wordCount implementations
Any UI or API surface beyond the exported function and its test suite
Performance optimization beyond what the composed functions already provide
problemStatement: Callers who need to dispatch text to an AI provider currently have to manually chain three separate utilities — normalizeText, then chunkText, then compute word counts per chunk — to turn raw input into provider-ready pieces. There is no single entry point that expresses the "clean this text and give me provider-sized chunks" intent, leading to repeated boilerplate and the risk of callers skipping normalization or word-count annotation.
userValue: Developers integrating with AI providers get a single, obvious call that delivers chunked, word-counted, provider-safe text. This eliminates the cognitive overhead of remembering the correct utility sequence and prevents subtle bugs from omitted normalization or inconsistent word-count computation across call sites.
